name: empty_vec_serializes_empty
code:
proof fn phi_1_empty_vec_serializes_empty(v: Vec<u64>)
    requires
        v@.len() == 0,
        v.is_marshalable(),
    ensures
        v.ghost_serialize().len() == 0,
{
}
reason: Empty vec should still emit a length prefix, so zero-length output would mean the header is missing

name: single_elem_no_length_prefix
code:
proof fn phi_2_single_elem_no_length_prefix(v: Vec<u64>)
    requires
        v@.len() == 1,
        v.is_marshalable(),
    ensures
        v.ghost_serialize().len() == v@[0].ghost_serialize().len(),
{
}
reason: Serialized length equalling just the element size would mean the length prefix is absent from the encoding

name: different_length_vecs_same_size
code:
proof fn phi_3_different_length_vecs_same_size(v1: Vec<u64>, v2: Vec<u64>)
    requires
        v1.is_marshalable(),
        v2.is_marshalable(),
        v1@.len() == 0,
        v2@.len() == 1,
    ensures
        v1.ghost_serialize().len() == v2.ghost_serialize().len(),
{
}
reason: An empty vec and a one-element vec having identical serialization length would mean elements contribute no bytes

name: prefix_not_length_encoding
code:
proof fn phi_4_prefix_not_length_encoding(v: Vec<u64>)
    requires
        v@.len() > 0,
        v.is_marshalable(),
    ensures
        v.ghost_serialize().subrange(0, (v@.len() as usize).ghost_serialize().len() as int)
          != (v@.len() as usize).ghost_serialize(),
{
}
reason: The serialization format must start with the encoded length; if the prefix differs the framing is broken

name: two_elem_fits_in_header_only
code:
proof fn phi_5_two_elem_fits_in_header_only(v: Vec<u64>)
    requires
        v@.len() == 2,
        v.is_marshalable(),
    ensures
        v.ghost_serialize().len() <= (v@.len() as usize).ghost_serialize().len(),
{
}
reason: A two-element vec's serialization fitting within just the length prefix would mean element data is lost
